deficient_sections: Research report: Analyze the complex issue of liability allocation in accidents involving vehicles with advanced driver-assistance systems (ADAS) operating in a shared human-machine driving context. Your analysis should integrate technical principles of ADAS, existing legal frameworks, and relevant case law to systematically examine the boundaries of responsibility between the driver and the system. Conclude with proposed regulatory guidelines or recommendations.
SAE J3016 driving-automation levels and the human-machine boundary
NHTSA automated-driving-systems policy and crash-reporting order
Product-liability / negligence doctrine for ADAS manufacturers
Relevant ADAS / automated-feature crash-liability case law
Proposed regulatory guidelines and liability-allocation recommendations

corpus_credibility_disclosure.disclosure_note: Weighted-corpus gate (PG_SWEEP_WEIGHTED_CORPUS_GATE) ON: the corpus is ACCEPTED and its credibility is DISCLOSED (weighted, domain-aware) rather than refused on tier-mix. 205 source(s); source-weighted mean credibility 0.24. The corpus deviates from the pre-registered tier distribution; under the old gate this would have been refused (abort_corpus_approval_denied / abort_corpus_inadequate). That tier-COUNT refusal verified no individual claim — the per-claim faithfulness floor (strict_verify + the 4-role D8 release decision) remains the binding check and is unchanged. Lower-tier sources are disclosed as lower-credibility, not dropped.
